mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is compatible with the Kafka API, designed to be faster and simpler to operate without the need for ZooKeeper or JVM. It is ideal for developers looking for a lightweight solution for real-time data streaming. Key trade-offs include the absence of certain Kafka features due to its unique architecture.

steps.title: Add the Redpanda Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Redpanda Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add redpanda https://charts.redpanda.com

```

> This is the official repository for Redpanda Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Redpanda CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Redpanda to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/crds/redpanda.vectorized.io_redpandas.yaml

```

steps.title: Install Redpanda using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Redpanda in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --create-namespace --version 25.3.10

```

steps.title: Create a Redpanda Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource for the Redpanda cluster to manage its configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

```
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify Redpanda Cluster
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Redpanda cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss unless backups are made.

```bash

helm uninstall redpanda --namespace redpanda

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have backups of your data. This process includes draining the nodes, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade redpanda redpanda/redpanda --namespace redpanda --version 25.3.10

kubectl get redpandas -n redpanda

```

> Replace <node-name> with the name of your Redpanda node.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Redpanda service is not running or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Redpanda pods with 'kubectl get pods -n redpanda' and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No such host'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates a DNS resolution issue within the Kubernetes cluster.

**Fix:**
Verify your Kubernetes DNS settings and ensure that the Redpanda service is correctly defined.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for Redpanda.

**Fix:**
Increase the resource limits in your Redpanda deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Topic creation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the auto-create topics feature is disabled.

**Fix:**
Ensure that 'auto_create_topics_enabled' is set to true in your Redpanda configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance for topic partitioning and added support for tiered storage.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No migration steps are required when upgrading from previous versions.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME           READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE' followed by your Redpanda pods indicating they are running.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vectorized.io/v1beta1
kind: Redpanda
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: redpanda
spec:
  replicas: 3
  configuration:
    auto_create_topics_enabled: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
